issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The default browser focus outline is removed globally using :focus-visible { outline: none } and :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none }. While a custom outline-offset is defined, no visible replacement (like a box-shadow, border, or new outline-color) is provided for all interactive elements, leaving keyboard users without a clear indication of which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 248: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide the content. Furthermore, the .sr-only-focusable class (intended for skip links) also uses position: static and width: auto, meaning the content is always visible in the layout rather than being hidden until focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 278: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em. Negative word-spacing causes characters and words to crowd together, significantly reducing legibility for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 168: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing to -0.05em. Tightening the space between letters makes text harder to decode, especially for users with dyslexia or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 169: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses a transform: translateY(-200%) and a transition on the transform property. There is no override within the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable this movement, which can cause vestibular distress.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; position: absolute; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button has an explicit min-height and min-width of 28px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 Level AA requirement of at least 24x24 pixels of target size, and below the 44x44 pixel recommendation for optimal mobile use.
issuesFound.fix: Line 518: min-height: 44px; Line 519: min-width: 44px;

issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary variable is set to #aaaaaa. When used against the white background (#ffffff) defined in --colour-neutral-0 (Line 14), the contrast ratio is 2.3:1, which is well below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 54: --colour-text-secondary: #636363;

issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration (underlines). While some buttons have background colors, standard links within body text (like those in .prose or footer) rely solely on color to be distinguished from surrounding text, which fails SC 1.4.1.

issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px. This makes the base rem unit equal to 12px, causing all text scaled via rem (like body text at 1rem) to be significantly smaller than the recommended 16px minimum for readability.

issuesFound.explanation: The p selector sets line-height: 1. This is too tight for paragraph text and fails the recommendation for a line height of at least 1.5 for body copy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 201: line-height: 1.5;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide the content. Furthermore, the .not-sr-only class (intended to hide content from everyone) also uses static positioning and visible dimensions, meaning content intended to be hidden remains visible and reachable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 286: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em. Negative word-spacing causes characters and words to crowd together, significantly reducing legibility for users with visual impairments or dyslexia.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing to -0.05em. Tightening the space between characters makes text harder to decode, especially at smaller font sizes or for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 187: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link component uses a transform: translateY(-200%) transition that triggers on focus. There is no override within the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable this movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button has an explicit min-height and min-width of 28px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of 24x24px only if there is sufficient spacing, but it fails the 44x44px recommendation for mobile-friendly targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 518: min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary is defined as #aaaaaa. When used on the default background --colour-bg-page (#ffffff), the contrast ratio is 2.3:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 130: --colour-text-secondary: #636363;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global 'a' selector removes text-decoration. While some links (like feature cards) add an underline on hover, many links (like those in the footer or navigation) rely solely on colour changes to indicate interactivity, which fails SC 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive colour differences.

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: font-size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px. This makes the base rem unit 12px, causing all text scaled via rem (like body text at 1rem) to be significantly smaller than the recommended 16px minimum for body text.

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: line-height below 1.5
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph (p) elements have line-height: 1. This is extremely cramped and violates the best practice of 1.5 for body text, making it difficult for users with visual or cognitive disabilities to track lines.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle CSS classes and scroll locks but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `_trigger` element. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation drawer has changed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1459: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function in the `productFilter` module updates the visual grid/list layout and internal state but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the button that was just clicked. Only the non-clicked buttons are updated in the subsequent loop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: `const btn = e.target.closest('[data-view-mode]'); if (!btn) return; setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product view mode is toggled between 'grid' and 'list', the visual layout changes, but no audible announcement is made to screen readers confirming the new view mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2421: `addClass(_gridEl, CSS.listView); removeClass(_gridEl, CSS.gridView); liveRegion.announce('Switched to list view');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module correctly adds error classes and links the error message via `aria-describedby`, but it fails to set the `aria-invalid="true"` attribute on the field. Assistive technologies will not perceive the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3115: `else { srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is validated as successful, the `_renderResult` function removes error classes but does not reset or remove the `aria-invalid` attribute. A field previously marked invalid will remain flagged as invalid in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3109: `if (result.valid) { srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function sets up the mobile navigation trigger and drawer but does not initialise the `aria-expanded` state to `false`. Screen readers may not announce the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1510: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class and body scroll lock but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');` Line 1159: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTabClick` function updates the active category and CSS classes for product filter tabs but does not update the `aria-pressed` state. Users of assistive technology will not know which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1845: `btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true'); (and set others to 'false')`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the visible card count is updated in the DOM via `_updateCountBadge`, but no call is made to a live region to announce the new results count. Users who cannot see the screen will not know if the filter returned results.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1815: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module updates the error message text and visibility but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2715: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', result.valid ? 'false' : 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1215: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Click/mouse handlers on non-native elements without matching keyboard
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` binds `dragover`, `dragleave`, and `drop` events to a `div` (zoneEl) but lacks a `keydown` handler for the Enter or Space keys to trigger the file selection dialog, despite having `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open(), close(), and toggle() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1770: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line 1790: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`. Line 1800: Update toggle() to set the correct state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on their trigger buttons. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1940: Inside _openInstance(inst), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line 1960: Inside _closeInstance(inst), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set an initial aria-expanded state on the navigation toggle button. The button's expanded state is unknown to screen readers until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1765: After obtaining the _trigger element in init(), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menus do not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init() function registers dropdown instances but does not set initial aria-expanded="false" on closed dropdown triggers. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1940: In the register() function, after setting up the trigger and panel, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates visible price displays and savings messages but does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology may not know the billing period has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: Inside setPeriod(period, animate), after updating the UI, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (or similar message based on period).

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid and count badge but does not announce the filter results to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology may not know the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: Inside applyFilters(), after calculating totalMatches, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied');`.

issuesFound.title: Viewport reveal animations not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The viewportReveal._reveal() function animates elements into view but does not provide any announcement for screen readers. Users who cannot see the animations may miss content becoming visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3010: Inside _reveal(el), add a conditional announcement, e.g., `if (el.hasAttribute('data-reveal-announce')) { liveRegion.announce('Content revealed'); }`.

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animations not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator._animate() function animates numeric counters but does not announce the final value to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology may not know the counter has reached its target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3110: Inside _animate(el), in the onComplete callback, add `liveRegion.announce(`Counter updated to ${cfg.target}`);`.

Stat counter animations not announced

Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded state of the navigation, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: .btn defines a transition that includes transform, and the :active state applies translateY(1px). No prefers‑reduced‑motion rule disables this motion, causing unwanted animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 702: .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } 
  Line 549‑551: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (translateY)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of the primary CTA button moves upward with transform: translateY(-1px) and is animated via the .btn transition. No reduced‑motion override disables this movement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1502: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } 
  Line 549‑551: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (rotate/scale)
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image animates on hover with 3‑D rotate transforms, using a transition defined on .hero-image img. No reduced‑motion media query disables this animation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the outline and does not provide any alternative visible focus style such as a box‑shadow or border. Keyboard users lose a clear indication of which interactive element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing required ARIA state attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) that opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer never receives `aria‑expanded` or `aria‑controls`. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or which element it controls.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not expose selection via `aria‑pressed` (and lack `aria‑controls`)
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector buttons (`[data-period]`) are used as a radio‑style group, but the script only toggles a CSS class. No `aria‑pressed` (or `aria‑controls` linking to the price panel) is set, leaving the current period invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period switch (`[data-billing-toggle]`) missing `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off toggle that switches between monthly and annual billing is treated as a switch (role="switch") but its pressed state is never reflected with `aria‑pressed`, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which billing period is active.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button with id `#scroll-top-btn` is made visible/invisible but never receives an `aria-label`. Keyboard‑only users navigating to the button receive no context about its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Product‑count badge does not announce changes to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The element `[data-product-count]` is updated with the number of visible products, but it lacks an ARIA live region. Users of screen readers are not informed when the count changes after filtering.

issuesFound.title: Changing the billing period is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the user selects a different billing period, the UI updates prices, but no ARIA live region is used to announce the change (e.g., “Annual billing selected”). This violates WCAG 2.2 (ARIA‑live).

issuesFound.title: ARIA‑expanded not initialized on page load for the mobile navigation drawer (duplicate of Issue 1 but at load time)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden on load, yet the trigger button does not have `aria‑expanded="false"` until the first interaction. WCAG requires the state to be present on load.
issuesFound.fix: (see Issue 1 – set the attribute to `false` during `mobileNav.init`).

fpTitles: ARIA‑expanded not initialized on page load for the mobile navigation drawer (duplicate of Issue 1 but at load time)

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions of the mobile navigation component add/remove CSS classes and lock scrolling, but they never set the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Assistive technologies therefore cannot know whether the navigation drawer is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed the script only shows/hides the panel and updates CSS classes. It never updates `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item)` function expands the answer panel and adds the visual “open” class, but it never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the question button. Consequently, screen‑reader users cannot detect that the item is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds error‑state CSS classes and shows an error message, but it never toggles the `aria‑invalid` attribute on the form field. Assistive technologies therefore do not receive the required error state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button that opens the mobile navigation drawer never receives an aria‑controls attribute pointing to the drawer element, so screen‑reader users cannot discover the relationship between the control and the region it toggles.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger aria‑expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads the trigger button has no aria‑expanded attribute (it should be false because the drawer is hidden). This violates the requirement that ARIA state be set before any user interaction.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module’s _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions add/remove CSS classes but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving assistive technology unaware of the menu’s state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger aria‑expanded not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads the dropdown trigger buttons lack an aria‑expanded attribute (they should start as false because the menus are hidden). This violates the “initialise ARIA state” rule.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion module’s open() and close() functions toggle CSS classes and animate height but never set aria‑expanded on the question button, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether an answer is visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger aria‑expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads the FAQ question buttons lack an aria‑expanded attribute (they should be false because all answers are collapsed). This violates the requirement to initialise ARIA state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons missing aria‑pressed initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the period buttons have no aria‑pressed attribute (they should reflect the default period). This violates the “initialise ARIA state” rule.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not update aria‑expanded when suggestion list opens
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module opens the suggestion list (adds hidden attribute removal) but never toggles aria‑expanded on the search input, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that a listbox is now visible.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() adds error messages and CSS classes but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the field, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the validator removes the error message but never removes aria‑invalid, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button never receives an aria‑controls attribute pointing to the drawer, so screen‑reader users cannot discover the relationship.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup component renders a <label> without htmlFor
issuesFound.explanation: The <label> element inside FormGroup does not have an htmlFor attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot associate the label with its input. This affects the newsletter subscription form.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">`

issuesFound.title: TextInput element missing an accessible label (newsletter email)

issuesFound.fix: Line 571: `<TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" aria-label="Email address" ... />`

issuesFound.title: TextInput element missing an accessible label (email address)

issuesFound.title: IconButton missing required aria-label (next carousel control)

issuesFound.title: <div> with onClick used as backdrop without role/button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose}> is clickable but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard handling, making it inaccessible via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1025: `<div role="button" tabIndex={0} className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" "){onClose();}}}>`

issuesFound.title: <div> overlay for modal used as click target without role/button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay `<div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}>` is interactive but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard handling, preventing keyboard users from dismissing the dialog via the overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1335: `<div role="button" tabIndex={0} className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" "){onClose();}}}>`

issuesFound.title: <nav> element for desktop navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation `<nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav">` has no aria-label or aria-labelledby, so screen‑reader users cannot distinguish it from other navigation landmarks.

issuesFound.title: <nav> element for mobile navigation body missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: <nav> element for skip‑links missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip‑links container `<nav>` does not have an aria-label, so its purpose is not announced to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735: `<nav aria-label="Skip navigation links">`

issuesFound.title: <section> element for hero missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero `<section className="hero-section">` has no aria-labelledby or aria-label, so screen‑reader users cannot identify the region.

issuesFound.title: Active desktop navigation link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: FAQ item button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Each FAQ accordion button toggles visibility of its answer panel but does not expose its state via aria-expanded, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of which items are open.

fpTitles: FAQ item button missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (Mobile navigation close)
issuesFound.explanation: The <IconButton> used to close the mobile navigation panel contains only an icon (✕) and does not provide an accessible name via aria-label, aria-labelledby or title. Screen‑reader users cannot perceive its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 274: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (Theme toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The <IconButton> that cycles the site theme displays only an emoji (☀️/🌙/💻) without an aria-label or other accessible name, making its function invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447: <IconButton aria-label="Toggle theme" onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (Testimonials carousel previous control)
issuesFound.explanation: The <IconButton> used as the “previous” control for the testimonials carousel contains only the “‹” character and lacks an accessible name, preventing screen‑reader users from understanding its action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (Testimonials carousel next control)
issuesFound.explanation: The <IconButton> used as the “next” control for the testimonials carousel contains only the “›” character and lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 627: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Disclosure button missing aria‑expanded (Nav dropdown trigger)
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> that toggles a navigation submenu has aria-controls and aria-haspopup but does not expose its expanded/collapsed state via aria-expanded, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the submenu state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 214: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} onClick={onToggle}>…

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation panel toggle missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav toggle <button> controls the off‑canvas navigation panel via aria-controls but does not indicate its expanded state with aria-expanded, so AT cannot convey whether the panel is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 390: <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>…

issuesFound.title: Mobile submenu toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands/collapses a mobile submenu uses aria-controls but lacks aria-expanded, so its state is not announced to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420:

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The “Monthly” and “Annual” buttons in <BillingToggle> visually indicate the selected period via CSS classes but do not expose the selected state with aria‑pressed, leaving screen‑reader users unable to determine the current billing period.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 545: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>Monthly</button>
  Fix: Line 551: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>Annual <Badge variant="success">Save 20%</Badge></button>

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter‑tab buttons in <FilterTabs> show the active filter via a CSS class but do not communicate the selected state to AT with aria‑pressed.
issuesFound.fix:
Within the map (line ~ 628), change the button to:
  <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>{tab.label}{...}</button>

issuesFound.title: Hero video play/pause button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> that toggles background video playback changes its icon between “⏸” and “▶” but does not expose its pressed state via aria‑pressed, so AT cannot convey whether the video is playing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 308: <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} aria-pressed={isPlaying} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">…

issuesFound.title: <label> elements not using htmlFor attribute (FormGroup)
issuesFound.explanation: The <label> rendered inside <FormGroup> does not have an htmlFor attribute linking it to the associated input, breaking the programmatic association required for form accessibility.
issuesFound.fix:
In FormGroup component (line ~ 822), change the label to:
  <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">{label}{required && (…)}</label>

issuesFound.title: Text inputs missing aria‑invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> receives an invalid prop but does not forward it as aria‑invalid, so assistive technologies are not informed of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In TextInput component (line ~ 842), add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the <input> element.

issuesFound.title: Required‑field visual marker announces a bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required asterisk  * is not hidden from AT, causing a screen reader to read “asterisk”. It should be hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 824: *

issuesFound.title: Multiple <nav> landmarks lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: There are three <nav> elements (desktop primary navigation, mobile navigation body, and skip‑link navigation) without aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, making it impossible for screen‑reader users to differentiate them.
issuesFound.fix:
Add descriptive labels, e.g.:
  Line 190: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Primary navigation" className="desktop-nav">…
  Line 447: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">…
  Line 115: <nav aria-label="Skip links">…

issuesFound.title: Section landmarks missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> elements for the hero and statistics bar are not labelled via aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, violating landmark naming requirements when multiple sections exist.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 298: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">…
  Fix: Line 514: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">…

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby references on form fields (NewsletterForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-describedby attribute references newsletter-email-error, but no element with that id exists, so error messages are not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Provide an ErrorMessage with matching id, e.g.:
  <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby references on contact form fields
issuesFound.explanation: Each input/textarea references an aria-describedby id (e.g., contact-name-error) that does not exist because the error message component is not rendered, breaking the association.
issuesFound.fix:
Add corresponding ErrorMessage components with matching id inside each <FormGroup>, e.g.:
  <ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: Multiple <section> elements without aria‑label/aria‑labelledby (additional sections)
issuesFound.explanation:
Only the hero and stats sections lack labeling; all other sections are correctly labelled. No further action needed beyond Issue 15.

(Note: Issues 16 and 17 address the same pattern across multiple fields; each field is reported separately as required.)

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only pattern.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` and `.hero-image img:hover` rules apply transform animations but lack a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow the motion. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 860: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none !important; transition: none !important; } }`
    Line 870: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card

issuesFound.fix:
Line 980: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transform: none !important; transition: none !important; } }`
    Line 990: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card:hover { transform: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonials carousel uses pointerdown/pointerup events for swipe detection but does not provide matching keydown handlers for Enter/Space to activate the same logic. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot trigger the swipe behavior, blocking access to carousel functionality.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function toggles the CSS class and scroll lock but never updates aria-expanded on the navToggle button. Screen readers cannot announce the open state.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the navToggle button at page load. Screen readers start with an ambiguous state.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.goTo() function updates the slide but never announces the new slide number or title to screen readers. Users who rely on screen readers cannot perceive the carousel change.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function adds/removes CSS classes and error messages but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Screen readers cannot announce the error state.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function removes the error message and CSS class on valid input but never sets aria-invalid="false" or removes the attribute. Screen readers continue to announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._onToggleClick() function toggles the billing period but never updates aria-pressed on the toggle button. Screen readers cannot announce the toggle state.

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.init() function does not set aria-pressed on the toggle button at page load. Screen readers start with an ambiguous state.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onPointerDown` and `_onPointerUp` handlers (lines 10503–10529) are attached to a non-native interactive element (the carousel root) to detect swipe gestures. These handlers respond to pointer events but do not include matching keyboard handlers for Enter or Space keys. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot trigger the swipe actions, creating a keyboard trap and violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions (lines 10134–10202) toggle the visibility of a dropdown panel but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the open/closed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function (lines 9840–9859) parses FAQ items and sets initial ARIA states but does not explicitly set `aria-expanded="false"` on trigger buttons for items that are initially closed. Screen readers may not announce the closed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` for items that are not initially open.
  Line 9855: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(item.open));

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge` function (lines 9680–9699) updates the product count badge text but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users who rely on screen readers cannot perceive the updated count, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix:
Add a live region announcement after updating the badge text.
  Line 9690: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} available`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_validateCurrentStep` function (lines 8980–9020) validates wizard steps but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields. Screen readers cannot announce the error state, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` for each invalid field in the errors array.
  Line 9005: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` function (lines 8800–8820) clears the error state but does not explicitly set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. Some screen readers may retain the error state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` in the `markValid` function.
  Line 8810: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The base :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining an outline-color, box-shadow, or border. This removes the visible focus indicator for all interactive elements that do not have a more specific override, making keyboard navigation impossible for sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule explicitly sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This ensures buttons have no focus indicator even if the base rule is fixed, blocking keyboard access.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on navigation toggle without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible replacement. This critical interactive element becomes invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have outline: none on focus-visible without a color, shadow, or border. Keyboard users cannot identify which link is focused in the footer.

issuesFound.explanation: The social media links have outline: none on focus-visible without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot perceive focus state on these interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger element does not manage aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (_trigger) changes the visibility of the nav drawer but never updates its own aria-expanded attribute. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed. This affects the open(), close(), and init() functions which manage this element.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger elements do not manage aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons toggle the visibility of their panels but do not update aria-expanded. This leaves screen reader users unaware of the dropdown state. The _openInstance, _closeInstance, and register functions all fail to set this attribute on inst.trigger or trigger.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where the trigger always reports "false" after any interaction cycle.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: Switching the billing period updates prices visibly but does not announce the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to screen readers. Users relying on AT will not know the prices they see correspond to a new billing cycle.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked as invalid, the error message is linked via aria-describedby, but the field itself is not marked with aria-invalid="true". Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid upon focus.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is validated as correct, the error description is removed, but aria-invalid is not cleared or set to "false". The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the mobileNav module toggle CSS classes and visibility but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed when the navigation menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1683: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1705: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not set initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button on page load. The initial state of the navigation menu is not communicated to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class selector sets outline: none but fails to provide a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator from all interactive elements throughout the site, making keyboard navigation impossible for sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on header logo link without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without adding a visible alternative focus style. Keyboard users cannot see when the logo link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on mobile navigation toggle without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. The mobile menu button becomes invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on main navigation links and buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Navigation items cannot be tracked by keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on submenu links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Dropdown menu links lack focus visibility for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on search submit button without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without adding a visible alternative. The search button becomes invisible when focused via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on account navigation links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Account links cannot be tracked by keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on feature card links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. The extended click area link lacks focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on filter tab buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Filter buttons cannot be seen when focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on pricing toggle buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Billing toggle buttons lack keyboard focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on table wrapper without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. The scrollable table region cannot be seen when focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on FAQ accordion buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Accordion triggers cannot be tracked by keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on form inputs without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Form fields lose focus visibility when navigated via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on checkboxes without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Checkboxes cannot be seen when focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer navigation links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Footer links lack keyboard focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on social media links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Social links cannot be tracked by keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer brand links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Brand links in the footer lack focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. High contrast mode users lose all focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which keeps the content fully visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. Content intended only for assistive technologies is displayed to all users.

issuesFound.title: Button touch target below minimum size (small button)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this button.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. The icon-only button is too small for reliable activation.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. The checkbox is difficult to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Button touch target below recommended size (base button)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. While above the 24px minimum, users with motor impairments may still have difficulty.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle selector sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. The hamburger menu button may be difficult to activate.

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu item touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Navigation items are smaller than recommended.

issuesFound.title: Submenu link touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Dropdown menu links are smaller than recommended.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. The search button is difficult to tap.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Filter buttons are smaller than recommended.

issuesFound.title: Product card button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-body .btn-secondary selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Product call-to-action buttons are smaller than recommended.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Billing toggle buttons are smaller than recommended.

issuesFound.title: Social link touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Social media links are smaller than recommended.

issuesFound.title: Skip link transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector includes transition: transform var(--transition-base) which causes spatial movement, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override is provided to disable this animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Button transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement on interaction, but lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transition: transform var(--transition-slower) and transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg), but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 920-925 is empty and does not disable the transform.

issuesFound.title: Product card transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card selector includes transition: ... transform var(--transition-base) and transform: translateY(-3px) on hover, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override is provided to disable this spatial animation.

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card hover transform missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector sets transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This makes text harder to read, particularly for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed globally without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none globally, removing underlines from all links. Without additional non-color visual differentiators (such as border-bottom or font-weight) in the base state, links may be indistinguishable from surrounding text for colorblind users.

issuesFound.title: Root font size below 16px reduces readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, which is below the 16px (1rem) recommended minimum for body text. This reduces readability for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator entirely, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on header logo link
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible replacement focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the logo link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 456: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on navigation toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. This mobile menu button lacks a focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 544: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.fix: Line 649: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on filter tab buttons

issuesFound.fix: Line 966: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on pricing toggle buttons

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social media links

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. Users in Windows High Contrast mode lose all focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348-350: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width and height to 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small checkboxes.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on icon-only buttons

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
