issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px, which meets the minimum but falls below the 44px recommended touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1364: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Content remains visible in the layout while also being announced by screen readers, creating a confusing experience. The class should use position: absolute and 1px dimensions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 364-373: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform properties and transitions that cause spatial movement (perspective rotation and hover lift). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable these animations, which can cause vestibular disorders in sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 772-775: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap at larger font sizes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 316: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces space between characters. While subtle, negative letter-spacing can reduce readability for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 317: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links inherit text-decoration: none from the global a rule and rely solely on color changes (from neutral-400 to neutral-0) to indicate interactivity. Users with color vision deficiencies cannot distinguish these links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1366: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

fpTitles: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
Negative letter-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the mobileNav module toggle the navigation drawer visibility by adding/removing CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but they never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1853: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` at the end of the open() function before the emit call.
    Line 1875: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` at the end of the close() function before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions manage dropdown panel visibility but fail to set aria-expanded="true" or "false" on the trigger element. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1952: In _openInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`.
    Line 1968: In _closeInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _open() and _close() functions in the siteSearch module show and hide the suggestion listbox but do not toggle aria-expanded on the search input element. The input has role="combobox" and aria-controls, but lacks the required aria-expanded state to indicate whether the suggestions are visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2450: In _open, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_searchState.open = true;`.
    Line 2462: In _close, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function in inlineValidator calls srUtils.markInvalid() for invalid fields and srUtils.markValid() for valid fields, but neither utility function sets the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers cannot identify which fields have failed validation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3105: Replace the else block content with `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`.
    Line 3110: Replace the if block content with `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in srUtils only manages aria-describedby relationships but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid control. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to identify validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1205: Add `setAttr(control, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside the markInvalid function after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error message from aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, leaving screen readers thinking the field is still invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1218: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` inside the markValid function after updating aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The init() function in mobileNav finds the trigger button but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state, even though the menu starts in a closed state. This violates the requirement that ARIA states must reflect the initial state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1905: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The register() function in dropdownMenu sets up trigger and panel elements but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on the trigger, even though the panel starts with the hidden attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1995: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The init() function in siteSearch configures the input with role="combobox" and aria-controls but fails to set the initial aria-expanded="false" state, even though the suggestion list starts hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2520: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates the billing period and UI state but never updates the aria-checked attribute (or aria-pressed) on the toggle element to reflect the current state. The element has role="switch" but lacks a dynamic aria-checked attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2835: In setPeriod, add `if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');` (or use ARIA.PRESSED if keeping as a button).

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic content not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the product grid and count badge when filters change, but does not announce the results to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed how many products match the current filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3320: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.filterApplied || 'Showing {n} products').replace('{n}', totalMatches));`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price updates visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2835: In setPeriod, after updating the UI, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens or closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. The announceToSR parameter exists in the function signature but is never used to trigger a live region announcement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1853: In open(), add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`.
    Line 1875: In close(), add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus does not trigger any live region announcement, leaving screen reader users unaware that a menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1952: In _openInstance, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`.
    Line 1968: In _closeInstance, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions accept an announceToSR parameter but never use it to announce the expanded or collapsed state to screen readers, even though STRINGS.faqOpened and STRINGS.faqClosed exist in the codebase.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3505: In open(), add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.faqOpened || '{title} expanded').replace('{title}', item.question));`.
    Line 3525: In close(), add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.faqClosed || '{title} collapsed').replace('{title}', item.question));`.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop completion not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _onClick handler in scrollToTop scrolls the page to the top and moves focus, but does not announce the action completion to screen readers, leaving users uncertain if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2160: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');` after the scrollToY call.

fpTitles: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
faqAccordion state change not announced

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels are shown and hidden without updating the aria-expanded attribute on their trigger buttons. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether a dropdown is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdowns are registered, the trigger button does not receive an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute even though the panel is hidden by default.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2560 (in register function, after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');): 
      setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion close function sets aria-expanded="false", but the open function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers unaware that the panel has expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4220 (in open function): Add after `item.open = true;`
      setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input does not have aria-expanded="false" set initially when the page loads and the suggestion list is closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2940 (in init function, after setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');): 
      setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing custom switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The custom billing toggle switch has role="switch" but never updates its aria-checked attribute to reflect the current state (monthly vs yearly).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3858 (in _syncToggleState function): Add after `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear);`
      setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter tab buttons show active state via CSS classes but do not update aria-pressed to indicate the currently selected filter to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation functions mark fields as invalid by adding error messages and CSS classes, but fail to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear it on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable but never initialises it with createFocusTrap. The condition `if (_trap && _trap.isActive())` in the open() function always evaluates to false, so focus is never trapped within the open navigation drawer, allowing keyboard users to tab out of the menu.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge updates visually but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving blind users unaware of how many results match their selection.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3520 (in _updateCountBadge function): Add after `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', ...);`
      liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing price updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled, prices update visually with animation, but the new price is not announced to screen readers, so users are not informed of the cost change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3750 (in _updatePlanCard function, after price display update): Add
      liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target} per ${period}`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions accept an announceToSR parameter but never use it to announce the navigation state change to screen readers. Users are not informed when the menu opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the mobileNav module toggle the navigation drawer visibility by adding/removing CSS classes but never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger), leaving screen reader users unaware whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2443: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
  Line 2473: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialise aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function discovers the trigger button and drawer elements but fails to set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger when the menu is closed by default, violating WCAG requirements that ARIA states must reflect the initial state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2425: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in the dropdownMenu module show and hide dropdown panels by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger) to reflect the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2623: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line 2658: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialise aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets up aria-haspopup and aria-controls attributes on trigger buttons but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" when the dropdown panels are hidden by default, causing a mismatch between the visual state and the accessibility tree on initial page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2768: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module manages a combobox pattern where _open() and _close() functions toggle the visibility of the suggestion listbox, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the search input (_inputEl) to indicate the expanded/collapsed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3118: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line 3148: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch does not initialise aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init() function configures the search input with role="combobox", aria-controls, and aria-haspopup, but fails to set the initial aria-expanded="false" state when the suggestion panel is closed by default, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3278: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function adds error message references via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field, preventing assistive technologies from programmatically identifying the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1342: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes error message references from aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for screen reader users after it becomes valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1375: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` immediately after the existing logic that updates aria-describedby (or alternatively `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable and deactivates it in close(), but the open() function never creates the focus trap using createFocusTrap() or activates it, leaving keyboard users unable to cycle focus within the mobile menu when it opens.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge() function in the productFilter module updates the visible product count text content but does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region, failing WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages for dynamic content updates that indicate filtering results.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3558: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);` immediately after `_countBadge.textContent = String(count);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates the billing period and visible prices but does not announce the change to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the content has changed when they select a different billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3860: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to {period} billing').replace('{period}', period));` immediately after `requestAnimationFrame(() => { _pricingState.animating = false; });`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop to provide an accessible name for screen reader users, but ThemeToggle renders IconButton without this prop, making the theme toggle button inaccessible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but the close button in MobileNav is rendered without it, leaving screen reader users without context for the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1084: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but the previous slide button is rendered without it, making the carousel navigation inaccessible to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1384: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name

issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button manages an open/close state but does not communicate this state to assistive technology via aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether the submenu is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup using wrong error prop name
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects the prop 'errorMessage' but receives 'error', causing the error message not to display and breaking the form's accessibility for screen reader users who rely on the error text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1568: Change error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} to errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button uses aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to screen reader users. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing whether the submenu is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1190: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button controls a panel via aria-controls but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the answer panel is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2050: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop per its TypeScript interface, but none is provided. This creates an icon-only button with no accessible name for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1350: Add aria-label prop: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button contains only the "‹" character and lacks an aria-label, providing no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2010: Add aria-label: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only the "›" character and lacks an aria-label, providing no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2015: Add aria-label: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button for the mobile navigation panel contains only the "✕" character and lacks an aria-label, making it inaccessible to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1270: Add aria-label: `<IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing toggle button visually indicates selection via CSS classes but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1920: Add aria-pressed: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button visually indicates selection via CSS classes but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1930: Add aria-pressed: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>`

issuesFound.title: TextInput component not propagating aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not apply it to the input element as aria-invalid, so screen readers cannot announce the invalid state of form fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~830: Add aria-invalid: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Textarea component not propagating aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not apply it to the textarea element as aria-invalid, so screen readers cannot announce the invalid state of form fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~850: Add aria-invalid: `<textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Select component not propagating aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not apply it to the select element as aria-invalid, so screen readers cannot announce the invalid state of form fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~870: Add aria-invalid: `<select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} >`

issuesFound.title: Section component ignoring labelledby prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a `labelledby` prop but does not apply it as aria-labelledby to the rendered element, preventing sections from being properly labelled as landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~890: Add aria-labelledby: `<Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} >`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class is broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto — the exact opposite of what a screen-reader-only utility should do. Content using this class is fully visible to sighted users and cannot serve its purpose of hiding content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat label
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) on a gradient background of #5588bb to #bb88ee. The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 1.5:1, far below the required 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision cannot read the stat labels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 863-866: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon button

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width: 20px and height: 20px, both below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty checking boxes.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition and 3D perspective rotation on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 695-698 is empty and does not disable the motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience disorienting 3D transforms.
issuesFound.fix: Line 695-698: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card motion missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover has transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) but this specific selector is not covered by the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block which only targets .pricing-card:hover. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience motion on the featured card.
issuesFound.fix: Line 903-905: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover, .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em which reduces the space between words. Negative word-spacing can make text harder to read, especially for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 173: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links. Without a non-colour visual differentiator such as bold text or border-bottom, links may be indistinguishable from surrounding text, violating SC 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 197: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Base font size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) baseline. This causes all rem-based text to be smaller than intended, reducing readability for users with low vision or reading difficulties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 165: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1858: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the collapsed state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1887: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger element does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" on page load. The ARIA state attribute must reflect the current state when the page loads, not only after interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1915: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function opens a dropdown panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1980: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function closes a dropdown panel but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element. Screen readers cannot announce the collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1997: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger, the function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialize aria-expanded="false". The ARIA state must reflect the initial closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle with role="switch" does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle element is assigned role="switch" in init() but _syncToggleState() never updates aria-checked. For role="switch", aria-checked is required to indicate the on/off state to screen readers, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2707: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function only adds aria-describedby to link the error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot announce that the field has an error, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1233: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not set aria-invalid="false"
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error description but never sets aria-invalid="false" or removes the attribute. The invalid state persists incorrectly for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1256: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2480: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` (add after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function removes CSS classes to hide the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element. Screen readers cannot announce the collapsed state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2500: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (add after `removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger element does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial state of the collapsible navigation, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2550: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (add after `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerKeydown);`)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function shows the dropdown panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2625: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` (add after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...)`)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function hides the dropdown panel but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element. Screen readers cannot announce the collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2635: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (add after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...)`)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the trigger element does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false". Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2705: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (add after `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`)

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function expands the FAQ panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4285: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` (add after `item.open = true;`)

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-button triggers lack Enter/Space keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: The _onKeydown handler only handles arrow keys for navigation but does not handle Enter or Space to toggle non-button triggers. Elements with role="button" require Enter/Space activation per WCAG 2.1.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4395: Add `case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); toggle(item); break;` (inside the switch statement before `default: break;`)

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function adds aria-describedby to link the error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1653: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` (add after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`)

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error description but never clears aria-invalid from the field. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid after the error is resolved.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1663: `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` (add inside the function body)

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle has role="switch" set in init() but _syncToggleState() never updates aria-checked. Switch widgets require aria-checked to reflect state per WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4035: `if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(period === 'year'));` (add inside _syncToggleState function)

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and visible products change, no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the result count change, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3615: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(cat) || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);` (add after `_syncPagination(totalMatches);`)

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, prices update visually but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the period change, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4090: `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` (add after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...)`)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav._onTriggerKeydown() does not handle Enter/Space for non-button triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The keydown handler only handles Escape key. If the nav toggle is not a native button element, Enter and Space keys will not activate it, violating WCAG 2.1.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2523: Add `if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); toggle(); return; }` (add at the beginning of the function body)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open function adds CSS classes and emits events but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the navigation menu has expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1912: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation close function removes CSS classes and emits events but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the navigation menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1935: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger element does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" on page load. Screen readers may announce an incorrect initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1980: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open function shows the panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the dropdown has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2070: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown close function hides the panel but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the dropdown has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2084: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger, aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" even though the panel starts hidden. Screen readers may announce an incorrect initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2110: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the line setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function expands the panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3245: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation error marking function adds aria-describedby to link the error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1590: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, aria-invalid is not cleared or set to "false". The invalid state persists even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1600: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` at the start of the function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce filter results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied and the visible count changes, no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2820: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(cat) || \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes (monthly to annual), no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3570: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...)`
